text: Two small end-to-end test runs of the openreview systematic-review pipeline, each deliberately sized to 50-100 PubMed records before filtering. The first built a melatonin-and-sleep-quality review (data/reviews/melatonin-sleep-quality/): two complementary queries deduped to ~100 records, dual-independent title/abstract screening across 8 concurrent agents (kappa=0.96), adjudication of 9 borderline cases, open-access full-text acquisition (only 19 of 41 got real full text), full-text screening, and per-arm extraction of 38 included studies (42 arms) into records.json, then views and report. During adjudication a real vocabulary gap surfaced â€” active-comparator-only trials had no matching exclusion reason â€” so a no-eligible-comparator reason was added to that review's protocol. The second run repeated the whole funnel for zinc and the common cold (data/reviews/zinc-common-cold/) from a single title query (~83 records, kappa=0.94/1.0), landing 14 included studies. Both reviews were deleted at the user's request once verified, leaving only the bundled mouse-genetics-mesothelioma example; the pipeline (search, screen, adjudicate, fetch, extract, validate, report) ran cleanly throughout with dual-independent screening and kappa agreement checks confirmed as the working pattern.
